patches.find: WIN-001 claims a fundamental resonance of 11.78 Hz while WIN-002 claims 7.83 Hz (with 26% aetheric damping reducing from 10.6 Hz). These are incompatible: if the disc thickness resonance is 11.78 Hz, the Schumann frequency should be near that value, not 7.83 Hz. The model claims credit for two different frequencies from two incompatible calculations. The selective damping signal. If the 26% aetheric damping factor from WIN-002 is applied consistently, Tesla's claimed 11.78 Hz should become 11.78 × (1 − 0.26) = 8.72 Hz. This frequency appears nowhere in the dome model — it is neither the claimed 7.83 Hz Schumann resonance nor the claimed 11.78 Hz Tesla resonance. The dome applies the damping factor to one calculation (WIN-002) but not the other (WIN-001), the diagnostic signature of ad hoc fitting: parameters applied selectively to preserve preferred values rather than derived from a unified physical model. The incompatibility also reveals that aetheric damping is applied selectively: if the 26% damping (WIN-002) were applied consistently, Tesla's 11.78 Hz should reduce to approximately 8.72 Hz. This frequency appears nowhere in the dome model. The 26% figure is applied to Schumann to make it match observation (10.59 Hz → 7.83 Hz) but is not applied to WIN-001's Tesla resonance — demonstrating that the damping constant is a post-hoc fitting parameter, not a physical property of the aether medium. The selective damping signal. If the 26% aetheric damping factor from WIN-002 is applied consistently, Tesla's claimed 11.78 Hz should become 11.78 × (1 − 0.26) = 8.72 Hz. This frequency appears nowhere in the dome model — it is neither the claimed 7.83 Hz Schumann resonance nor the claimed 11.78 Hz Tesla resonance. The dome applies the damping factor to one calculation (WIN-002) but not the other (WIN-001), the diagnostic signature of ad hoc fitting: parameters applied selectively to preserve preferred values rather than derived from a unified physical model. This frequency appears nowhere in the dome model — it is neither the claimed 7.83 Hz Schumann resonance nor the claimed 11.78 Hz Tesla resonance. The dome applies the damping factor to one calculation (WIN-002) but not the other (WIN-001), the diagnostic signature of ad hoc fitting: parameters applied selectively to preserve preferred values rather than derived from a unified physical model.

patches.rationale: Remove two duplicate copies of selective-damping-signal paragraph and internal restatement. Keeps one clean version. Triplicated text was trivial target for dome defenders citing sloppy editing.

patches.find: the phase velocity of the fundamental mode of a spherical cavity resonator with Earth’s radius.
patches.replace: the phase velocity of the fundamental mode of a spherical cavity resonator with Earth’s radius. Identically, the dome’s disc radius r_disc = 20,015 km equals π×R_earth = 20,015.09 km to four decimal places — Earth’s half-circumference under the polar azimuthal equidistant projection. Neither va nor r_disc is a dome-derived quantity; both are globe quantities in disc clothing.
patches.rationale: Add explicit r_disc=πR_earth identity sentence. r_disc=20,015 km = π×6371 = 20015.09 km to 4 decimal places. The strongest forensic datapoint that disc radius is Earth half-circumference relabeled.

patches.find: The confirming value comes from a field-specific published dataset (IGRF, NASA station archives, ocean-tide gauges, telluric records, etc.) that was publicly available at the time of the dome monitor commit
patches.replace: The confirming value comes from Tesla’s 1899 Colorado Springs Notes and US Patent 787412 (1905), both in the public record for over a century
patches.rationale: Replace generic IGRF/NASA/ocean-tide/telluric boilerplate with actual WIN-001 confirming source: Tesla 1899 Colorado Springs Notes and US Patent 787412. None of the boilerplate datasets apply to WIN-001.

patches.replace: On a flat disc, the natural reading of 2diametrically opposite’ is reflection through the pole — which for Colorado Springs places the target near the Antarctic rim. No flat-disc interpretation produces 12,717 km.
patches.rationale: Soften "no clean geometric meaning" to specify natural disc interpretation (pole reflection → Antarctic rim) before landing the 14,300 km figure. Removes rhetorical gap defender could exploit.

patches.find: The paper explicitly states 'no significant anomaly during the very solar eclipse was found.' The dome model’s −6.5 μGal comes from this study but misrepresents its timing: gravitational shielding would peak at maximum eclipse (maximum coverage), not at the contacts. A subsequent reanalysis (Yang & Wang, 2002, Astrophys. Space Sci. 282, 245–253) found the anomaly evidence weaker than the initial report once additional measurement controls were applied.
patches.replace: The dome model’s −6.5 μGal comes from this study but misrepresents its timing: gravitational shielding would peak at maximum eclipse (maximum coverage), not at the contacts. A subsequent reanalysis by the same lead observer (Yang & Wang, 2002, Astrophys. Space Sci. 282, 245–253) found the anomaly evidence weaker once additional measurement controls were applied, and explicitly states ‘no significant anomaly during the very solar eclipse was found.’
patches.rationale: Re-attribute "no significant anomaly during the very solar eclipse was found" from Wang et al. 2000 to Yang & Wang 2002. The 2000 paper claims shielding-consistent signal; the 2002 reanalysis walks it back. Matches WIN-012 attribution. Adds DOI hyperlink for Yang & Wang 2002.

patches.find: Both coefficients match the observed WMM pole strengths precisely because they were fitted to them, not derived from dome geometry.
patches.replace: Both coefficients were fitted to observed WMM pole strengths — the southern coefficient (C=64,852 nT) matches to within ∼3%, but the northern coefficient (A=62,376 nT) is ∼11–15% above the observed NMP strength (∼54,000–56,000 nT). Neither value was derived from dome geometry.
patches.rationale: Remove misleading "precisely" from tldr_evidence. North-pole coefficient A=62,376 nT is 11-15% above observed NMP (~54-56k nT). South-pole C=64,852 nT matches within ~3%. "Precisely" is false for the north pole match.
Upgrade plain-text §6.1 reference in tldr_verdict to clickable anchor, matching format in expanded detail text (c5 patch).
